title: neoptychocarpus
side_effects: Neoptychocarpus can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its high alkaloid content. Prolonged use can also lead to cardiovascular problems, including high blood pressure and irregular heartbeats, in sensitive individuals. Furthermore, improper use of neoptychocarpus may cause allergic reactions, such as skin rashes and respiratory issues in people with pre-existing conditions. In severe cases, improper use can even lead to neurological damage, including seizures and coma in rare instances.
